definition: A non-obvious transmission pathway where the Fed embeds AI productivity gains in lower r* estimates before gains materialize, leading to premature easing, with the equity wealth channel amplifying demand and making last-mile disinflation harder. Identified as the most original insight across all analyses.
evidence: Fed may embed AI productivity in lower r* estimates before productivity gains materialize
Equity wealth channel operates in reverse (amplifying demand effect)
Historical Solow paradox/Brynjolfsson J-curve pattern supports the installation-before-productivity sequence
open_questions: How explicitly is the Fed incorporating AI productivity assumptions into r* estimates?
What is the magnitude of the equity wealth channel offset to monetary easing?
